input : fields n (F p)
h_input : Vector.map (Expression.eval env) input_var = input
h_assumptions : True
h_holds :
  Vector.map (Expression.eval env) (Vector.mapRange n fun i => var { index := i₀ + i }) =
    Vector.map (Expression.eval env) (Vector.mapFinRange n fun i => Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r))
⊢ Vector.map (Expression.eval env) (Vector.mapRange n fun i => var { index := i₀ + i }) = Vector.rotate input r

failed_attempts: exact h₁⟩
h

take a concrete example. Let `n = 3`, `r = 1`, `i₀ = 0`:
- `h_holds` becomes: `[var 0, var 1, var 2]_eval = [var 1, var 2, var 0]_eval`, because `Fin.ofNat 3 1 = 1`, and `i + 1` for `i ∈ {0,1,2}` gives `1,2,0`.
- Goal: `[var 0, var 1, var 2]_eval = [var 1, var 2, var 0]_eval`, which is correct.
But if `i₀ = 1`, `h_holds`: `[var 1, var 2, var 3]_eval = [var 1, var 2, var 0]_eval`, and the goal: `[var 1, var 2, var 3]_eval = [var 1, var 2, var 0]_eval`, which again is fine. 

But now, why is `h_holds` true? 
It seems that `var (i₀ + i)` is being mapped to

            have h₆ : ((i.val + r) : ℕ) % n < n := Nat.mod_lt _ (NeZero.ne' n)
            exact h₆
            ⟩ : Fin n)) := by

        rw [h₅]
        have h₆ : Vector.map (Vector.get input) (Vector.mapFinRange n fun i => (⟨((i.val + r) : ℕ) % n, by

                  ⟩ : Fin n)) := by rfl

have h₁ : Vector.map (Expression.eval env) (Vector.mapFinRange n fun i => Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r)) = Vector.rotate input r := by
      calc
        Vector.map (Expression.eval env) (Vector.mapFinRange n fun i => Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r)) = Vector.map (Expression.eval env) (Vector.mapFinRange n fun i => Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r)) := rfl
        _ = Vector.map (fun x => Expression.eval env x) (Vector.mapFinRange n fun i => Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r)) := rfl
        _ = Vector.map (fun x => Expression.eval env x) (Vector.mapFinRange n fun i => Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r)) := rfl
        _ = Vector.rotate input r := by
          -- Prove that the rotated input vector matches the expected output
          have h₂ : ∀ i < n, Expression.eval env (Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r)) = Vector.get input ((i + r) % n) := by
            intro i hi
            have h₃ : i < n := hi
            have h₄ : (i + Fin.ofNat n r : ℕ) = (i + r) % n := by
              simp [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.ofNat_val]
              <;>
              (try cases n <;> simp_all [Nat.mod_eq_of_lt]) <;>
              (try omega) <;>
              (try ring_nf at * <;> omega)
              <;>
              (try
                {
                  have h₅ : r < n := by
                    have h₆ : r < n := by
                      by_contra h₆
                      have h₇ : r ≥ n := by omega
                      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.ofNat_val]
                      <;> omega
                    omega
                  omega
                })
              <;>
              (try
                {
                  omega
                })
            have h₅ : Expression.eval env (Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r)) = Vector.get input ((i + r) % n) := by
              have h₆ : Vector.map (Expression.eval env) input_var = input := h_input
              have h₇ : Vector.get (Vector.map (Expression.eval env) input_var) ((i + r) % n) = Vector.get input ((i + r) % n) := by
                rw [h₆]
              have h₈ : Vector.get (Vector.map (Expression.eval env) input_var) ((i + r) % n) = Expression.eval env (Vector.get input_var ((i + r) % n)) := by
                simp [Vector.get_map]
              have h₉ : Expression.eval env (Vector.get input_var ((i + r) % n)) = Vector.get input ((i + r) % n) := by
                linarith
              have h₁₀ : (i + Fin.ofNat n r : ℕ) = (i + r) % n := h₄
              have h₁₁ : Expression.eval env (Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r)) = Expression.eval env (Vector.get input_var ((i + r) % n)) := by
                simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
                <;>
                (try omega)
              linarith
            exact h₅
          -- Now, prove the main equality using the above lemma
          have h₃ : Vector.map (fun x => Expression.eval env x) (Vector.mapFinRange n fun i => Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r)) = Vector.rotate input r := by
            calc
              Vector.map (fun x => Expression.eval env x) (Vector.mapFinRange n fun i => Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r)) = Vector.map (fun x => Expression.eval env x) (Vector.mapFinRange n fun i => Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r)) := rfl
              _ = Vector.map (fun i => Expression.eval env (Vector.get input_var (i + Fin.ofNat n r))) (Vector.mapFinRange n id) := by simp [Vector.map_map]
              _ = Vector.map (fun i => Vector.get input ((i + r) % n)) (Vector.mapFinRange n id) := by
                apply Vector.map_congr
                intro i _
                rw [h₂ i (Fin.is_lt _)]
              _ = Vector.map (fun i => Vector.get input ((i + r) % n)) (Vector.mapFinRange n id) := rfl
              _ = Vector.rotate input r := by
                -- Prove that the mapped vector is the rotated input vector
                have h₄ : ∀ i < n, Vector.get (Vector.map (fun i => Vector.get input ((i + r) % n)) (Vector.mapFinRange n id)) i = Vector.get (Vector.rotate input r) i := by
                  intro i hi
                  have h₅ : i < n := hi
                  have h₆ : Vector.get (Vector.map (fun i => Vector.get input ((i + r) % n)) (Vector.mapFinRange n id)) i = Vector.get input ((i + r) % n) := by
                    simp [Vector.get_map, Vector.mapFinRange, Fin.val_mk]
                    <;>
                    (try omega)
                  have h₇ : Vector.get (Vector.rotate input r) i = Vector.get input ((i + r) % n) := by
                    simp [Vector.rotate_get, Vector.get]
                    <;>
                    (try omega)
                  rw [h₆, h₇]
                -- Use the above lemma to prove the equality of vectors
                have h₅ : Vector.map (fun i => Vector.get input ((i + r) % n)) (Vector.mapFinRange n id) = Vector.rotate input r := by
                  apply Vector.ext
                  intro i hi
                  apply h₄
                  <;> simp_all [Fin.is_lt]
                  <;> omega
                rw [h₅]
          exact h₃
    simp_all
